pattern: Greedily assign the least-frequent available character to each '?' slot, using a min-heap over (count, char) to stay lexicographically minimal.
hints: The value of the final string equals sum of cost(i) over all positions, where cost(i) is the count of s[i] that appeared before index i — this is minimized by distributing replacements as evenly as possible.
Count existing character frequencies, then for each '?' pick the character with the current lowest frequency (using a min-heap keyed by (count, char) for lex ordering).
Collect the characters chosen for '?' positions, sort them, and fill the '?' slots left-to-right to ensure lexicographic minimality among equally-valued strings.

edge_inputs.description: one question mark with existing chars
question marks and all same existing char
lex smallest requires sorted assignment, not greedy fill
multiple chars with same count - lex order of char decides

stress.description: random string with mixed letters and question marks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    chars = list(string.ascii_lowercase) + ['?'] * 10
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(size))
    return [s]
